title: Stop Uniface Servers and Routers from the Uniface Router Monitor
breadcrumbs: Stop Uniface Servers and Routers from the Uniface Router Monitor
text: Stop Uniface Servers and Routers from the Uniface Router Monitor If you are a privileged user, you can stop a Uniface Server from the Router Monitor using its Server ID. You can also stop more than one server by specifying matching criteria for servers or matching criteria for clients that are being served. Note: When a Uniface Server connects to the Uniface Router, it is assigned a unique number. This number is the Server ID displayed in the Servers List. To stop servers: Select Servers >Stop . The Stop servers form is displayed. Specify the stop mode and identify the servers to be stopped. Table 1. Stop Server Options Field Description Stop mode Forced —servers are stopped immediately and clients are disconnected. The Uniface Server is stopped irrespective of its state. The stop message is sent to the Uniface Server, and subsequent client messages are not forwarded. Normal —existing clients are allowed to deliver requests to servers until the server reports it has no state, that is, it has no locks and no context (active component instances). At that moment, the server is stopped. Stop server with specific ID ID of the server to stop. When a Uniface Server connects to the Uniface Router, it is assigned a unique ID number which you can see in the Servers list form. Stop servers matching profile Stop servers serving clients matching profile. Enter some or all of the following matching criteria for servers or clients (the field contents are case-sensitive): Host —host name of the machine on which the servers or clients are running. User —user name under which the servers or clients are running. UST —Uniface Server Type (UST) of the servers or clients. The criteria you enter must be whole words; you cannot use Uniface-style profiles, such as * or ? . Stop servers serving clients matching profile Click OK . To stop the Uniface Router: Choose URouter >Stop URouter . The Stop URouter form is displayed. Specify the stop mode and enter the message displayed to new clients and monitors that are refused connections. Click OK . Related Links Uniface Router The Uniface Router is a multi-threaded process (urouter) responsible for interprocess communication in Uniface applications. It listens for requests from Uniface client applications and routes them to the appropriate Uniface Server, and it passes messages between various Uniface applications. Uniface Router Monitor The Uniface Router Monitor is a Uniface Windows application that you can use to fine-tune the performance of the Uniface Router while it is running. Uniface Server A Uniface Server is a server-based process that handles requests for remote data and component execution that it receives from client applications. Acquire Privileged User Status for the Uniface Router Monitor If you have privileged user status, you can use the Uniface Router Monitor to stop and start Uniface Servers, change Uniface Router settings, change and add Uniface server type (UST) settings, stop the Uniface Router, and reload the Uniface Router’s assignment file.
